overview: Bloomburrow is a charming creature-centric set where all creatures are animals. The format is synergy-driven with strong tribal themes. Each color pair has a distinct animal type identity, and the key mechanic Offspring allows creatures to create token copies. The format is relatively fast with excellent aggro options but rewards well-built synergy decks.

mechanics.name: Offspring
mechanics.description: When you cast a creature with Offspring, you may pay an additional cost to create a 1/1 token copy of it.
mechanics.draft_tip: Offspring is excellent value. The token copy keeps all abilities, making creatures with strong ETB effects or keyword abilities premium Offspring targets.

mechanics.name: Valiant
mechanics.description: Valiant triggers whenever the creature becomes the target of a spell or ability you control for the first time each turn.
mechanics.draft_tip: Valiant rewards combat tricks and targeted effects. Draft cheap targeting spells like equipment and auras to trigger Valiant repeatedly.

mechanics.name: Forage
mechanics.description: To Forage, you exile three cards from your graveyard or sacrifice a Food token.
mechanics.draft_tip: Forage needs graveyard fuel. Self-mill and card draw help keep your graveyard stocked. Food tokens provide an alternative cost.

mechanics.name: Expend
mechanics.description: Expend triggers when you spend a certain total amount of mana during your turn (Expend 4, 6, or 8).
mechanics.draft_tip: Expend rewards ramping and casting multiple spells. Higher Expend thresholds need careful mana planning — don't try to hit Expend 8 without ramp.

mechanics.name: Gift
mechanics.description: Some spells let you give an opponent a gift (usually a token or card). If you do, the spell has an enhanced effect.
mechanics.draft_tip: The gifted benefit to your opponent is usually minor compared to what you get. Most Gift cards are worth using with the gift option.

archetypes.name: Birds Flyers
archetypes.description: White-Blue focuses on Bird creatures and flying synergies to dominate the air.
archetypes.strategy: Draft flyers aggressively. Use tempo spells to protect your attackers and outrace ground-based strategies.

archetypes.name: Rats Sacrifice
archetypes.description: Blue-Black uses Rat tribal with sacrifice and recursion themes.
archetypes.strategy: Generate Rat tokens and sacrifice them for value. Use recursion to grind and removal to control the board.

archetypes.name: Lizards Aggro
archetypes.description: Black-Red Lizards is the premier aggressive archetype with excellent low-curve creatures and burn.
archetypes.strategy: Curve out with aggressive Lizard creatures. Use burn and removal to clear blockers and close games by turn 6-7.

archetypes.name: Raccoons Expend
archetypes.description: Red-Green Raccoons use the Expend mechanic to get value from spending mana on multiple spells.
archetypes.strategy: Ramp early, then chain spells together to hit Expend thresholds. Raccoon payoffs reward you for casting multiple spells per turn.

archetypes.name: Rabbits Go-Wide
archetypes.description: Green-White Rabbits goes wide with token generation and anthem effects.
archetypes.strategy: Generate Rabbit tokens through Offspring and token makers. Use anthem effects to pump your team and overwhelm opponents.

archetypes.name: Bats Lifegain
archetypes.description: White-Black Bats combines flying pressure with lifegain synergies.
archetypes.strategy: Use Bat tokens and lifegain to stabilize while building aerial pressure. Lifegain payoffs provide extra value.

archetypes.name: Otters Spells
archetypes.description: Blue-Red Otters rewards casting noncreature spells with prowess-like triggers.
archetypes.strategy: Draft a mix of efficient creatures and cheap spells. Chain spells together for maximum trigger value.

archetypes.name: Squirrels Forage
archetypes.description: Black-Green Squirrels use Forage to generate value from the graveyard.
archetypes.strategy: Fill your graveyard through trading and self-mill. Use Forage to convert graveyard resources into board advantage.

archetypes.name: Mice Valiant
archetypes.description: Red-White Mice uses Valiant triggers with combat tricks for explosive turns.
archetypes.strategy: Draft cheap Mice with Valiant and plenty of targeting effects. Combat tricks do double duty — triggering Valiant and winning combat.

archetypes.name: Frogs Ramp
archetypes.description: Green-Blue Frogs ramps into large threats and uses card advantage to outvalue opponents.
archetypes.strategy: Ramp into expensive threats while using card draw to maintain resources. Frogs reward patience and bigger mana.

general_tips: Offspring is the best mechanic in the set — creatures with strong Offspring abilities should be drafted highly.
The format is faster than average. Have a plan for the first four turns or you'll fall behind.
Carrot Cake is one of the best commons in the set — it provides Food, card filtering, and a body.
Tribal synergies are important but don't force a tribe. Good cards are better than on-tribe mediocre cards.
Combat tricks are better than usual due to Valiant — they trigger abilities and win combat.
Food tokens are valuable both for lifegain and as Forage fuel. Don't sacrifice them casually.
